raw_consumer_statement:
At the end of XXXX, I updated my payment information with American Express ( AMEX ) without realizing that it defaults to " Statement Balance. '' On XX/XX/XXXX, I woke up to find my bank account overdrawn by {$3500.00}. I immediately called AMEX, and they informed me the only solution was to issue a check, which would take over a week to process. I contacted my bank, which confirmed that I didnt have enough funds to cover the transaction so it would fail. I then called AMEX back to cancel the check, and they agreed, telling me to wait for my account to update so I could make the minimum payment. 

However, on XX/XX/XXXX, AMEX attempted the payment again, overdrafting my account by {$3500.00}. I called AMEX again, and this time, I was told that their policy is to retry failed ACH payments up to three times, and there was nothing they could do to stop this. They advised me to place a stop payment with my bank, which I did. 

Meanwhile, I was still waiting for my AMEX account to update so I could make the minimum payment. Finally, on XX/XX/XXXX, my account was updated, allowing me to make a minimum payment. However, AMEX processed the check payment despite our previous agreement to cancel it and I never received any check so in my mind I thought this was canceled, and now my outstanding balance has ballooned to over {$7000.00} when I initially owed only {$3500.00}. 

AMEX 's handling of this situation has been unprofessional. They failed to provide complete information, and their customer service representatives did not listen or update my account properly. Despite being a large company, AMEX seems to lack proper processes for handling requests and communicating effectively. Even my bank has advised me to switch to a different credit card company due to how poorly this was managed.
